name: Mason Rudolph
headline: Steelers veteran backup with no standalone value
body:
Rudolph remains exactly what his decade in the league suggests: a competent emergency starter, not a fantasy asset. In 2025 he played just five games on 52 attempts, completing 73.1 percent for 310 yards, 2 touchdowns and 2 picks with a tiny 1.5 completed air yards per completion and a 9.3 percent deep-attempt rate. His 0.063 EPA per play and 5.8 CPOE flatter a checkdown-heavy sample; the 15 fantasy points and rank of 55 tell the real story.

The more telling baseline is 2024 in Tennessee, when eight starts produced 1,530 yards, 9 touchdowns against 9 interceptions, a -0.006 EPA per play and just 95.8 points (QB37). A 2018 third-rounder out of Oklahoma State, he profiles as a pocket passer with no rushing floor (7 carries, 6 yards in 2025) and a 5.7-game-per-year availability pattern. Behind Pittsburghs starter, Rudolph carries zero redraft value and belongs on the waiver wire in all formats unless an injury hands him a job.
